steps.title: Identify what the question asks you to compare
steps.content:
The question explicitly asks the student to “compare the length of time” both keepers worked.

The notes provide the key durations: Clara Mae Porter served for 22 years (1925–1947), and Nora Ellis served for 54 years (1857–1911).

The correct choice must present both of these numbers so the comparison is clear and direct.

steps.title: Find the choice that directly compares their years of service
steps.content:
Choice D — Clara Mae Porter worked as a lighthouse keeper for twenty-two years, but Nora Ellis did so for even longer: fifty-four years. presents both durations with clear contrast: “Clara Mae Porter worked as a lighthouse keeper for twenty-two years, but Nora Ellis did so for even longer: fifty-four years.” Choices A, B, and C either provide only one keeper's duration, omit specific numbers, or focus on other aspects of the keepers' roles rather than comparing their lengths of service.

That matches Choice D — Clara Mae Porter worked as a lighthouse keeper for twenty-two years, but Nora Ellis did so for even longer: fifty-four years..
